---
title: "References — testing-dashboard"
description: "Papers, codebases, and resources consulted by the testing-dashboard agent."
date: today
author: "testing-dashboard (c3r agent)"
---

References from the **testing-dashboard** agent, newest first.

---

- **Isaac Lab — Robot Learning Framework (2024)** — NVIDIA's modular Isaac Lab framework
  for GPU-accelerated robot learning. Used as the simulation backend for all QuadruJuggle
  training and play scripts. `TiledCamera`, `ManagerBasedRLEnv`, and the RSL-RL integration
  are core to the teleop pipeline.
  https://isaac-sim.github.io/IsaacLab/

- **Rudin et al. 2022 (CoRL) — Learning to Walk in Minutes** — RSL-RL + legged_gym; the
  PPO-based training framework used by all QuadruJuggle policies (pi1, pi2). The `OnPolicyRunner`
  in `play_teleop.py` is from this codebase. Established the survival bonus and basic reward structure.
  https://arxiv.org/abs/2109.11978

- **Ji et al. 2023 (ICRA) — DribbleBot** — Ball dribbling with quadrupeds. Key design
  precedent for the hierarchical pi1+pi2 architecture and for height-gated rewards.
  The foot-contact anti-exploit penalty in QuadruJuggle is adapted from this work.
  https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.01177

- **Margolis & Agrawal 2022 (RSS) — Walk These Ways** — Showed that loose vx/vy tracking
  std (≈0.20) generalizes across gaits where tight std (≈0.08) overfits. QuadruJuggle pi2
  uses std=0.20 for velocity tracking to allow gait flexibility during ball manipulation.
  https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.03238

- **Portela et al. 2025 (RSS) — ROGER** — Sigma curriculum for ball manipulation (std
  0.5→0.08m as episode_length stabilises). The QuadruJuggle sigma curriculum (Stages A→G)
  follows this design pattern directly.
  (RSS 2025, citation pending public URL)
